hawkish_phrases: ongoing tightening of monetary policy over the forecast period...would be appropriate
excess demand rose above 1% of potential output by the end of the forecast period
CPI inflation picked up to above the 2% target in two years' time and was still rising
dovish_phrases: downside risks to growth have increased
the perceived likelihood of a no-deal Brexit has risen
Forward interest rates in major economies have fallen materially further
CPI inflation is likely to fall below the 2% target later this year
summary: The MPC voted unanimously to hold Bank Rate at 0.75%, maintaining that ongoing gradual tightening would be appropriate if the economy develops in line with May projections assuming a smooth Brexit, though acknowledging increased downside risks to growth from trade tensions and no-deal Brexit concerns.
